prompt:
You judge yoga teacher training (YTT) pages.

A visitor is deciding: "Should I enroll here, or keep looking?"

Five facts must be near the top, before any story:
- WHEN — dates
- WHERE — place
- PRICE
- WHAT — what you will learn, hours, style
- HOW LONG — days or weeks

Good page:
- All 5 facts visible before any philosophy
- Real curriculum with topics listed
- Teacher names and short bios
- Reviews or past student outcomes
- Clear way to apply or book

Bad page:
- "What is yoga" or philosophy before the price
- "Why choose us" filler sections
- Price or dates hidden far down
- Many different training offers mixed on one page

SEO (Google) — check:
- Title names the course, hours, and city (e.g. "200hr YTT Rishikesh")
- Clear H1 matching the title
- Course schema, FAQ schema if there is a FAQ
- Real curriculum depth — Google rewards this on training pages
- Images with alt text, unique photos

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of what the page offers>",
  "conversionScore": "<1-10, how well does it help a visitor decide and enroll?>",
  "seoScore": "<1-10, how well can Google find and rank it?>",
  "trainingName": "<name of the training program>",
  "hours": "<total hours or duration>",
  "price": "<price or price range>",
  "dates": "<upcoming dates>",
  "style": "<yoga style>",
  "curriculumVisible": true/false,
  "teacherBiosVisible": true/false,
  "notes": "<one sentence assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
If the category description specifies a JSON schema, follow it exactly.
Otherwise, return fields you find relevant based on the category description.
Every record must always include "url" (the page URL) and "summary" (1-2 sentence plain-language overview of the page).
Fields ending in "Score" must be numbers from 1 to 10.
Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response:
{
  "records": [
    {
      "url": "https://www.shalastudios.com/academy",
      "pageName": "Sha-La Academy",
      "summary": "The Sha-La Academy in Berlin offers a broad roster of yoga teacher trainings and continuing education programmes, ranging from a 200h multi-style YTT to short specialist intensives in Yin, Kundalini, Katonah, and more, with dates, prices, and links to details all visible on one hub page.",
      "conversionScore": 7,
      "seoScore": 5,
      "trainingName": "Multiple programmes — flagship is 200h Multi-Style Yoga Teacher Training",
      "hours": "Ranges from 10h to 200h depending on programme",
      "price": "€150 – €3,500 (early bird discounts available across programmes)",
      "dates": "May 2026 – Jun 2027 (various programmes)",
      "style": "Multi-style (Vinyasa, Yin, Kundalini, Katonah, and more)",
      "curriculumVisible": false,
      "teacherBiosVisible": false,
      "notes": "The hub page surfaces all five key facts (dates, location, price, style, duration) efficiently for each programme, but curriculum detail and teacher bios require clicking through to sub-pages, and mixing 12+ unrelated offerings on one page risks overwhelming visitors trying to evaluate a single training."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.shalastudios.com/apprenticeship",
      "pageName": "Yoga Apprenticeship Program (404)",
      "summary": "This URL returns a 404 error — the Yoga Apprenticeship Program page has either been moved or deleted, leaving prospective students unable to access details or apply.",
      "conversionScore": 1,
      "seoScore": 1,
      "trainingName": "Yoga Apprenticeship Program",
      "hours": "Not available (page not found)",
      "price": "Not available (page not found)",
      "dates": "Not available (page not found)",
      "style": "Not available (page not found)",
      "curriculumVisible": false,
      "teacherBiosVisible": false,
      "notes": "Critical broken link — the academy hub page links to this URL but it returns a 404, meaning any visitor clicking through loses all momentum and the page passes no SEO value."
    }
  ]
}
